text:
We have two ropes, each takes 60 minutes to burn completely, but they burn unevenly.  
To measure 45 minutes:

1. Light rope A at both ends and rope B at one end simultaneously.  
2. Rope A will burn completely in 30 minutes (since both ends burn).  
3. At that moment, rope B has 30 minutes of burn time left (since it was burning from one end for 30 minutes).  
4. Now light the second end of rope B. It will then burn out in 15 more minutes.  
5. Total time from start: 30 + 15 = 45 minutes.

This uses both ropes, so the minimum number of ropes used is 2.

Final integer: 2
